messages.content:
Three ants walk on a thin rod 100 cm long.
When an ant reaches either end of the rod it falls off. When two ants meet,
they BOUNCE: both instantly reverse direction, and each keeps its own speed.

Starting positions and velocities (positive = toward the 100 cm end):

- Ant A: at 10 cm, moving +3 cm/s
- Ant B: at 40 cm, moving -1 cm/s
- Ant C: at 80 cm, moving -2 cm/s

After how many seconds does the LAST ant fall off the rod? Give the exact
number of seconds.


When you are finished, output your final answer on its own line, as the last line of your response, in the exact format:
ANSWER: <your answer>

text:
This is a bounce problem where the ants keep **their own** speeds, so the classic "bounces are equivalent to pass-throughs" trick doesn't apply (that only works when all speeds are equal). I'll simulate the events exactly.

**Event 1 — A meets B.** A: 10+3t, B: 40−t. Equal when 4t = 30, so t = 7.5 s at position 32.5 cm. They reverse: A now moves −3, B moves +1. C is at 65 cm moving −2.

**Event 2 — t = 55/3 s.** From t = 7.5: 
- A heads left from 32.5 at 3 cm/s, reaching 0 after 32.5/3 = 65/6 s → falls off at t = 7.5 + 65/6 = **55/3 s**.
- B (+1) and C (−2) close their 32.5 cm gap at 3 cm/s → meet after the same 65/6 s, at position 130/3 cm.

So exactly when A falls off, B and C bounce: B reverses to −1, C reverses to +2. They now move apart, so no further meetings.

**Fall-off times.**
- C: from 130/3 cm at +2 cm/s, needs (100 − 130/3)/2 = 85/3 s → off at 55/3 + 85/3 = 140/3 ≈ 46.67 s.
- B: from 130/3 cm at −1 cm/s, needs 130/3 s → off at 55/3 + 130/3 = **185/3 s ≈ 61.67 s**.

The last ant (B) falls off at 185/3 seconds.

ANSWER: 185/3
